title: See's Candies
lesson: A small business with durable brand affection and pricing power can compound unusually well when it needs little incremental capital.
avoid_when: brand is fading
growth requires heavy capital
customer switching behavior is changing

title: Coca-Cola
lesson: A simple product with global distribution, habit, and brand memory can be easier to understand than a technically superior but less durable business.
avoid_when: consumer tastes are structurally moving away
valuation already assumes perfection

title: American Express after the salad oil scandal
lesson: A temporary scandal can create opportunity if the core customer franchise and trust mechanism remain intact.
avoid_when: the crisis destroys the trust asset itself
liabilities are unknowable

title: GEICO
lesson: A structural cost advantage in insurance can be powerful when underwriting discipline and customer economics survive cycles.
avoid_when: growth comes from underpricing risk
loss reserves are uncertain

title: The Washington Post Company
lesson: A strong local or category franchise can be materially mispriced when the market underestimates its durable economics.
avoid_when: the distribution monopoly is being structurally broken

title: Berkshire textile operations
lesson: A statistically cheap asset can still be a bad business if it requires capital while lacking durable pricing power.
avoid_when: there is a real durable cost or product advantage

title: Dexter Shoe
lesson: Paying with valuable stock for a business with an eroding moat compounds the mistake on both sides.
use_when: consumer product exposed to low-cost competition
avoid_when: competitive advantage is demonstrably strengthening

title: Salomon Brothers crisis
lesson: When reputation is at risk, the institution has to act as if trust is the scarce asset, not quarterly profit.
avoid_when: the facts are not established
legal advice is required

title: Wells Fargo account scandal
lesson: A strong franchise can still be damaged when incentives normalize bad behavior and leadership detects it too slowly.
avoid_when: problem is isolated and incentives are already fixed with evidence

title: Airlines: long avoidance and later investment
lesson: A hard industry can occasionally look investable, but weak structure and capital intensity can still overwhelm attractive moments.
avoid_when: company has a true structural advantage not shared by the industry

title: Apple as a consumer franchise
lesson: A technology company may fit the framework when the key question is consumer behavior, ecosystem stickiness, and capital returns rather than predicting engineering change.
avoid_when: value depends on forecasting fast technical shifts beyond evidence
source_family: annual meeting discussions and public comments

title: IBM investment and exit
lesson: A business can look understandable from history while the competitive base is shifting faster than the model captures.
avoid_when: current economics show durable advantage and growth independent of legacy assumptions

title: Amazon and Google omission
lesson: Avoiding what you do not understand protects capital, but it can also create large omission errors.
avoid_when: the user wants hindsight certainty rather than a decision process

title: BNSF Railway
lesson: A capital-intensive business can still be attractive if the asset is hard to replicate, demand is durable, and reinvestment earns acceptable returns.
avoid_when: returns on required capital are poor or politically capped

title: Precision Castparts
lesson: Even a high-quality industrial business can disappoint if the purchase price and cycle assumptions are too optimistic.
avoid_when: normalized earnings and downside are well supported

title: Kraft Heinz
lesson: Brand value and cost discipline are not enough if consumer preferences and category economics are weakening.
avoid_when: brand is gaining share and pricing power with evidence

title: Nebraska Furniture Mart
lesson: An exceptional operator with a clear cost culture can be more important than a complicated corporate control system.
avoid_when: retail economics are moving online faster than the operator can adapt

title: Borsheims
lesson: In trust-heavy retail, reputation and customer treatment can be central assets, not soft language.
avoid_when: trust cannot be measured through repeat behavior or economics

title: Clayton Homes
lesson: When a business touches consumer finance and vulnerable customers, reputation and incentive design need extra scrutiny.
avoid_when: the user wants a one-sided defense without examining incentives

title: Insurance float at Berkshire
lesson: Float is valuable only when it is low cost or better and matched with disciplined underwriting over time.
avoid_when: loss reserves or tail risks are opaque

title: General Re
lesson: Complex risk books can hide leverage and cleanup costs even inside otherwise valuable institutions.
avoid_when: risk terms are transparent and conservatively reserved

title: USAir preferred stock
lesson: A security can have attractive terms while the underlying industry economics still make the decision fragile.
avoid_when: downside is protected by assets and industry structure is improving

title: Gillette
lesson: A small repeated consumer purchase with brand trust and distribution can be a powerful franchise.
avoid_when: private label or technology is eroding the habit

title: Moody's
lesson: Some franchises benefit from embedded industry position, but reputation and regulatory trust are part of the moat.
avoid_when: trust in the gatekeeper role is structurally impaired

title: PetroChina
lesson: A commodity or state-linked business can still be investable at the right price, but exit discipline matters when price catches value.
avoid_when: governance or political risk dominates the valuation

title: Tesco
lesson: When facts deteriorate, delay can turn an ordinary mistake into a larger capital allocation error.
avoid_when: new evidence clearly repairs the original thesis

title: Low-cost index funds for most investors
lesson: For most people, minimizing costs and avoiding false expertise can beat complicated active decisions.
avoid_when: user asks for personalized allocation or tax advice

title: Berkshire share repurchases
lesson: Repurchases make sense when done below a conservative view of value and when they improve remaining owner economics.
avoid_when: repurchases are used to offset dilution or flatter metrics

title: Cash and Treasury bills
lesson: Holding cash can be rational when opportunities are unattractive and liquidity has option value in stress.
avoid_when: cash is held from fear without a decision standard

title: Berkshire succession and Greg Abel
lesson: A durable institution needs culture, managers, and capital allocation principles that can survive the founder.
avoid_when: post-transition performance is being predicted without evidence

title: Giving Pledge and delegated philanthropy
lesson: Philanthropy can be treated as capital allocation where comparative advantage may lie with trusted operators rather than the capital owner.
avoid_when: the problem requires local expertise not present in the case
source_family: Giving Pledge and Gates Foundation materials
